definition: An ordered list of numerical components.
A coordinate position in a vector.
A measured or encoded property used in a vector.
A learned or engineered mapping from an object to vector space.
A vector space in which embedded objects are compared.
A measure of directional alignment between two vectors.
A measure combining vector alignment and magnitude.
An indexed vector closest or most similar to a query vector.
A system for storing and querying embeddings.
A method for projecting high-dimensional data into fewer dimensions.
A change in model behavior or domain behavior over time.
Evaluation of representational harms or skewed outcomes.
A risk where vector similarity is treated as human meaning

column6: and version metadata attached to a vector.
